rewritten: Where is the headquarters of Lotus Technology located?
pos_recalls: Lotus Technology Company (LTC) was incorporated as an exempted company in accordance with the laws and regulations of the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The mailing address of Lotus Technology's principal executive office is No. 800 Century Avenue, Pudong District, Shanghai, PeopleвҖҷs Republic of China, and the phone number is +86 21 5466 - 6258. Lotus Technology's corporate website address is www.group-lotus.com. The information contained in, or accessible through, Lotus Technology's website does not constitute a part of this prospectus.

Lotus Technology has a dedicated global team to support its R&D activities with a sizable scale and comprehensive functionalities, covering all major technological perspectives. Lotus Technology's approach to innovation demonstrates an interconnected global collaboration among highly experienced and dedicated teams in the U.K., Germany, and China, each with different technological focuses. Centered around Lotus Technology's global headquarters in Wuhan, China, which focuses on cloud computing and online data processing, the company operates a software center in Shanghai focusing on global system integration and network security, and a research institute in Ningbo focusing on electric architecture, charging and power systems, and intelligent driving. Lotus Technology has established two R&D centers on engineering and product design, including the Lotus Technology Creative Centre (LTCC) located in the heart of the British car industry in Coventry, U.K., with a focus on automotive design and design strategy, product and brand communication, and sustainable material science, and the Lotus Technology Innovative Centre (LTIC) located in Frankfurt, Germany, with a focus on behavioral science, innovative vehicle technologies, dynamic attribute development, user interface, and regional tuning and application.

In October 2023, Lotus Technology's wholly foreign-owned enterprise (WFOE) pledged the land use rights, buildings, and ancillary facilities on certain pieces of land totaling approximately 84,000 square meters where Lotus Technology's global headquarters is located in Wuhan, China, to the Jingkai Fund pursuant to an exchangeable note agreement entered into between the WFOE and the Jingkai Fund in September 2021. Such pledge was terminated in April 2024 following the listing of Lotus Technology's securities on Nasdaq pursuant to the terms of the pledge.

In October 2023, Lotus Technology's wholly foreign-owned enterprise (WFOE) pledged the land use rights, buildings, and ancillary facilities on certain pieces of land totaling approximately 84,000 square meters where Lotus Technology's global headquarters is located in Wuhan, China, to the Jingkai Fund pursuant to an exchangeable note agreement entered into between the WFOE and the Jingkai Fund in September 2021. Such pledge was terminated in April 2024 following the listing of Lotus Technology's securities on Nasdaq pursuant to the terms of the pledge. Lotus Technology maintains various insurance policies to safeguard the company against risks and unexpected events. Lotus Technology maintains property insurance, public liability insurance, commercial general liability insurance, employerвҖҷs liability insurance, driverвҖҷs liability insurance, and inland transit insurance. In addition to providing social security insurance for employees as required by applicable laws, Lotus Technology also provides supplemental commercial medical insurance for employees. Lotus Technology does not maintain business interruption insurance or key-man insurance. Lotus Technology believes that its insurance coverage is adequate to cover key assets, facilities, and liabilities.

Lotus Technology is currently building, and expects to continue to develop, its headquarters in Wuhan, on land over which it has acquired land use right certificates. Major construction projects, such as the construction of the headquarters in Wuhan, require significant capital and are subject to numerous risks and uncertainties, including delays, cost overruns, disputes with builders and contractors, construction quality issues, and safety considerations, which are factors that Lotus Technology cannot control. Any failure to complete these projects on schedule and within budget could adversely impact Lotus Technology's financial condition and results of operations. Under the laws of mainland China, construction projects are subject to broad and strict government supervision and approval procedures, including but not limited to project approvals and filings, construction land and project planning approvals, environmental protection approvals, pollution discharge permits, work safety approvals, fire protection approvals, and the completion of inspection and acceptance by the authorities. The construction projects being or to be carried out by Lotus Technology are undergoing necessary approval procedures as required by law.

rewritten: What is the main business of Lotus Technology?
pos_recalls: Lotus Technology is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance lifestyle electric vehicles and has set high quality and safety standards for and makes continuous improvements on its vehicles and technologies, including sensory hardware and ADAS software. Lotus Technology also plans to monetize its R&D capabilities by licensing its intellectual properties to other luxury auto brands and providing ADAS software subscriptions to its customers. Continue to launch new models and upgrade existing models. The successful launches of new models are critical for Lotus Technology to continue capturing market share in the luxury BEV market and strengthening its leadership position. Lotus Technology plans to launch a well-balanced portfolio of new models with exceptional performance in the coming years to broaden its customer base, in particular high net worth individuals and the tech-savvy younger generations, and expand its product breadth. Lotus Technology plans to launch two additional fully-electric vehicles over the next two years, including a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026. Lotus Technology also intends to upgrade its models to equip its vehicles with innovative technology and design. Focus on sustainability and lead in electrification.

the Joint Venture Agreement of Lotus Company between the Transferor and the Transferee вҖңBusinessвҖқ The business of Lotus Technology is to engage in activities within the automotive industry and related sectors that do not require public permits, as well as to provide advisory and other services, particularly in rendering research, production, and development activities. Lotus Technology is also engaged in research and development of automobiles and key components, as well as automotive engineering development services.

Lotus Technology Inc. has operations across the UK, the EU, and China. The company is dedicated to delivering luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicles, with a focus on world-class research and development in next-generation automobility technologies such as electrification, digitalization, and more. For more information about Lotus Technology Inc., please visit www.group-lotus.com.
Services. Lotus Technology generates revenues mainly by providing automotive design and development services to OEM customers, technology-related services, and after-sales service.

rewritten: What is the relationship between Lotus Technology and Lotus Cars?
pos_recalls: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand вҖңLotusвҖқ and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance sports car brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus UK and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker.

Lotus Technology believes that its close relationship with Geely Holding provides the company with a unique competitive advantage in its ability to rapidly scale commercialization while maintaining an asset-light operating model with less upfront capital expenditure commitment than other original equipment manufacturers (OEMs). Lotus Technology has entered into a variety of agreements, including agreements related to technology licensing, manufacturing cooperation, and supply of framework, among others, with Geely Holding. The strategic partnership with Geely Holding allows Lotus Technology to effectively control supply chain-related risks and accelerate product development. Lotus Technology's ability to successfully operate its Global Commercial Platform will affect its ability to increase revenues. Lotus Technology has taken steps to diversify its revenue sources, for example, by entering into the Distribution Agreement with Lotus UK, pursuant to which Lotus Technology is the exclusive global distributor (excluding the U.S., where Lotus Technology International Limited will act as the head distributor with the existing regional distributor continuing its functions) for Lotus Cars Limited to distribute Lotus UK vehicles, parts, and certain tools, and to provide aftersales services, branding, marketing, and public relations for such vehicles, parts, and tools distributed by Lotus Technology.

The method for selecting suppliers depends on the nature of the supplies needed. For general parts that are widely available, Lotus Technology examines proposals from multiple suppliers and chooses based on quality and price competitiveness, among other factors.

Proactive inventory management strategies resulted in a temporary impact on gross margin, but this was partially offset by the growth of the high-margin intelligent driving business. The gross margin of sales of goods declined to 7% for the first nine months of 2024, compared to 10% in the same period of 2023, mainly due to destocking. pos_recalls: Lotus Intelligent Driving research and development commenced as early as 2018. Lotus Technology supports end-to-end intelligent driving technologies with expertise in best-in-class hardware, advanced software and algorithms, and powerful cloud solutions. The deployable LiDAR technology is capable of all-around perception coverage with seven HD cameras of eight-megapixel, six long/short-range millimeter-wave radars, among which the front and rear are image radars, four 128-line LiDARs, 12 ultrasonic radars, one in-car camera, and four around-view cameras. в—Ҹ Pioneering 800-volt architecture. Lotus Technology has launched an 800-volt electric platform by doubling the voltage to meet customersвҖҷ high-power demand in battery electric vehicles (BEVs). The Eletre and Emeya come with two electric motors, one driving the front wheels and another driving the rear wheels. This motor also incorporates an 800-volt SiC inverter and two-speed gearboxes to achieve acceleration from zero to 100 km per hour within three seconds and to maintain strong vehicle power performance at the highest speed of 265 km per hour. в—Ҹ Supercharging feature. Underpinned by Lotus Technology's proprietary 800-volt architecture and high-power motors, the company has developed a 420 kW supercharging solution. The supercharging feature can electrify the battery level from 10% to 80% within 20 minutes and reach a range of 120 km with five minutes of charging time. Lotus Technology is improving the performance of its supercharging solutions to further reduce the charging time.

Lotus Technology has integrated these hardware, software, and cloud capabilities to develop a full stack of advanced intelligent driving technologies, which enable Lotus vehicles to perceive and intelligently react to their surroundings, thereby enhancing the driving experience. By combining powerful algorithms, HD cameras, radars, and high-precision mapping capabilities, Lotus vehicles are able to precisely perceive their surroundings in stereoscopic display. Lotus vehicles then dynamically react to their perceived surroundings by leveraging customized planning and control algorithms. Taken together, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving solution offers a smooth and intelligent driving experience to drivers, even under extreme road conditions. Lotus Technology has developed an electrical, instrumentation, and control (EIC) system, which enhances the efficiency and performance of battery electric vehicle (BEV) models. With the EIC system, Lotus Technology is pioneering the release of 800-volt architecture and incorporating high-power motors into the e-mobility system. rewritten: What is the company's view on the global luxury electric vehicle market? What is the company's perspective on industry competition?
pos_recalls: However, the global luxury BEV market is currently underserved, with only approximately 10 existing luxury BEV models, compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models, leaving consumers with limited choices. As an early mover in the global luxury BEV market, Lotus Technology is leading the electrification transformation of this fast-growing luxury car segment, launching its E-segment BEV model years ahead of competitors and targeting to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. Lotus Technology launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, Eletre, in 2022. Beginning with Eletre, all new car rollouts will be BEV models. Lotus Technology expects to capture market share and realize first mover advantages by addressing unfilled demands in the current market. Eletre is a luxury lifestyle E-segment SUV powered by Lotus Technology's 800-volt Electrical Performance Architecture, or EPA, which is a newly debuted self-developed BEV platform initially based on the same foundation of Sustainable Experience Architecture, the worldвҖҷs first open-source BEV architecture. Combining its technologically advanced platform with cutting-edge design, Eletre delivers leading performance in acceleration, driving range, and charging speed.

The Lotus brand is closely associated with вҖңcustomer engagementвҖқ and вҖңcommunity building,вҖқ and in order to provide customers with a luxurious experience, Lotus Technology has adopted a direct-to-consumer global sales and distribution strategy that focuses on establishing and developing direct relationships with customers, especially in selected regions including China, Europe, and the U.S., which represent some of the key areas driving fast growth in the global battery electric vehicle markets. By offering a luxurious purchasing experience and superior customer service, Lotus Technology expects to further deepen its penetration in the global market and aims to establish a total of over 300 stores globally by 2025. Develop next-generation electric vehicle technologies while monetizing Lotus TechnologyвҖҷs R&D prowess. Lotus Technology intends to continuously develop and enhance proprietary cutting-edge technologies, including the 800-volt EPA architecture and hardware, algorithm, and software systems to enhance the competitiveness of its vehicles, supported by continuous R&D investment. All of Lotus Technology's proprietary technologies are built with the principle of вҖңFor the Drivers,вҖқ while inheriting its unique design language and philosophy.

Competition Lotus Technology faces competition from both traditional luxury automotive developers and an increasing number of newer companies focused on electric and other alternative fuel vehicles. Lotus Technology believes the primary competitive factors on which Lotus Technology competes with its peers include, but are not limited to: в—Ҹ brand recognition, prestige, and heritage; в—Ҹ design, styling, and luxury; в—Ҹ technological innovation; в—Ҹ driver experience; в—Ҹ product quality and performance; в—Ҹ product reliability and safety; в—Ҹ battery range, efficiency, and charging speeds; в—Ҹ customer service and customer experience, including access to charging options and availability and terms of aftersales services; в—Ҹ product pricing; в—Ҹ management team experience in bringing electric vehicles and other disruptive technologies to market; в—Ҹ manufacturing efficiency; в—Ҹ environmental impact and perception; в—Ҹ the degree and sophistication of related vehicle software. Lotus Technology believes that the company is favorably positioned to compete on the basis of these factors. However, many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors have substantially greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than the company. These competitors may be able to deploy greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sales, marketing, and support of their products.

Many of Lotus Technology's current and potential competitors, particularly international competitors, have significantly greater financial, technical, manufacturing, marketing, and other resources than Lotus Technology does and may be able to devote greater resources to the design, development, manufacturing, distribution, promotion, sale, and support of their products. Lotus Technology expects competition in the automotive industry to intensify in the future in light of increased demand and regulatory push for alternative fuel vehicles, continuing globalization, and consolidation in the worldwide automotive industry. Factors affecting competition include, among others, local protectionism, brand recognition, product quality and performance, technological innovation, product design and styling, pricing, safety, and customer service. Increased competition may lead to lower vehicle unit sales and increased inventory, which may adversely affect Lotus Technology's business, financial condition, operating results, and prospects. The company's ability to successfully overcome the industry barriers and compete in its market will be fundamental to its future success in existing and new markets and its market share. There can be no assurance that Lotus Technology will be able to successfully overcome the industry barriers and compete in its markets.

These competitors also compete with Lotus Technology in recruiting and retaining qualified research and development, sales, marketing, and management personnel, as well as in acquiring technologies complementary to, or necessary for, its products. Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in these competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, its focus on design and experience, its advanced technologies, its relationship with Geely Holding and therefore the benefits it can obtain during the vehicle development phase, and its future-proof approach give it a competitive edge and allow it to formulate a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that it believes generates significant competitive advantages, allowing it to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

The global automotive market is highly competitive and has historically been associated with significant barriers to entry, including large capital requirements and investment costs for developing, designing, manufacturing, and distributing vehicles, long lead times to bring vehicles to market from the concept and design stage, the need for specialized design and development expertise, regulatory requirements, establishing a brand name and image, and the need to establish sales and service locations. Lotus Technology has strategically entered into the sustainable luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, and the company faces a variety of added challenges that a traditional automobile manufacturer would not encounter, including additional costs of developing and producing an electric powertrain that has comparable performance to a traditional gasoline engine in terms of range and power, inexperience with servicing electric vehicles, regulations associated with the transport of batteries, and the need to establish or provide access to sufficient charging locations and unproven high-volume customer demand for fully electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the sustainable luxury BEV market segment to become even more competitive in the future as additional players enter this segment. The company competes with competitors all around the world.

The growth of the global luxury car segment is expected to outpace that of the overall car market, growing at a CAGR of 10% from 2021 to 2031, according to Oliver Wyman. New York вҖ“ Oct 17, 2024 вҖ“ Lotus Technology Inc. (вҖңLotusвҖқ or the вҖңCompanyвҖқ), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its preliminary vehicle delivery results for the first nine months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 7,543 vehicles in the first nine months of 2024, representing a year-on-year increase of 134%. Global allocation in terms of deliveries remained well-distributed, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of the World regions each contributing about 20-35% of the total deliveries. In the third quarter of 2024, the Emeya (GT, Sedan) was delivered to Europe, whilst the Eletre (SUV) reached markets including the GCC, Japan, and Korea. In September, Lotus Technology Inc. revealed the Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market with a starting price of $229,900. The deliveries of the Eletre Carbon are expected to commence in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter.

How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

rewritten: Does Lotus Tech own the Lotus brand?
pos_recalls: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand вҖңLotusвҖқ and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

Lotus Technology Inc. (вҖңthe CompanyвҖқ), an exempted company with limited liability, was incorporated in the Cayman Islands on August 9, 2021. The Company, through its wholly owned subsidiaries, consolidated variable interest entity (вҖңVIEвҖқ) and VIEвҖҷs subsidiaries (collectively, вҖңthe GroupвҖқ), is primarily engaged in the design, development, and sale of luxury battery electric lifestyle vehicles (вҖңBEV lifestyle modelsвҖқ) under the вҖңLotusвҖқ brand (вҖңLotus BEV businessвҖқ). The Group is also a distributor that sells luxury sports cars under the вҖңLotusвҖқ brand (вҖңLotus sports car businessвҖқ) across the world. Additionally, the Group provides other sales and ancillary services to customers. On February 22, 2024 (the вҖңClosing DateвҖқ), Lotus Technology consummated its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (вҖңLCAAвҖқ) pursuant to a merger agreement dated January 31, 2023 (as amended and restated dated as of October 11, 2023, the вҖңMerger AgreementвҖқ) by and among LCAA, Lotus Technology, a Cayman Islands exempted company, Lotus Temp Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (вҖңMerger Sub 1вҖқ), and Lotus EV Limited, a Cayman Islands exempted company and wholly-owned subsidiary of Lotus Technology (вҖңMerger Sub 2вҖқ).

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total deliveries of 7,617 units, representing a 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Global allocation was relatively diversified, with each region contributing 18-35% of the total deliveries in the first nine months of 2024, powered by over 200 stores in prime locations worldwide. The European market continued to expand, with a year-on-year increase of 372% in deliveries during the first nine months of 2024. The Rest of the World region achieved a year-on-year growth of 110% as Lotus Technology continued to make steady progress in its expansion into new markets. In the third quarter of 2024, the deliveries of the Emira commenced in South Africa and India, and of the Eletre in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. The Eletre Carbon, a high-performance variant tailored for the North American market, was revealed and is expected to commence deliveries in the first half of 2025. Deliveries of bespoke and collection versions of both the Eletre (SUV) and the Emeya (GT, Sedan) also commenced in the third quarter. Lotus continues to drive technological innovation to meet market demand.

In the first nine months of 2024, Lotus delivered over 7,600 vehicles, marking a 136% increase compared to the same period in 2023, when 3,221 vehicles were delivered. The third quarter alone saw 2,744 vehicles delivered, a 54% increase over the 1,782 vehicles delivered in Q3 2023. Total revenue for the first nine months of 2024 amounted to $653 million, representing an impressive 105% year-on-year growth from the $318 million reported in the same period of 2023. In the third quarter of 2024, revenue was $255 million, showing a 36% increase from the $188 million in Q3 2023. The intelligent driving business showed remarkable growth, with revenue from external customers reaching $11 million, reflecting a year-on-year growth of 450%. Despite this, the gross profit margins declined, with Q3 2024 margins at 3%, down from 15% in Q3 2023. For the first nine months of 2024, the gross profit margin was 9%, compared to 11% in the same period of 2023.

The 900V Hyper Hybrid EV technology features a Hybrid Electric Drivetrain and dual Hyper Charging technology: Ultra-Fast Plug-In Charging and Ultra-Fast On-The-Drive Charging, both with industry-leading charging speeds. The Hyper Hybrid technology enables high performance, high efficiency, and a flexible longer driving distance of over 1,100 kilometers.  
в—Ҹ Delivery of Urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) Service and OTA: Lotus started open beta testing for urban NOA in key cities in October 2024, following the launch of highway OTA services. Certain OTA features, including the highway assistant driving function, have been launched in Europe.

Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand вҖңLotusвҖқ and sells all Lotus-branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further positioned the company as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer.

Lotus Technology collaborates with a battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturing facility in Wuhan, China, which is owned and operated by Geely Holding. This manufacturing plant is purpose-built for electric vehicles and incorporates advanced manufacturing technologies. Covering an area of over one million square meters, the plant has the capacity to produce up to 150,000 vehicles per year. The facility has been constructed as a brand-new, world-class plant for the production of electric vehicles. The plant is equipped to conduct stamping, welding, painting, and assembly, and features testing tracks, a quality inspection center, and a utility power and sewage treatment center. It includes an advanced system that allows vehicles to be transported into workshops using intelligent driving technology without any human intervention. The facility is also equipped with an approximately three-kilometer-long track for quality inspection. Customers can experience various driving activities such as racing, drifting, and off-roading on the track. The track includes nine left turns and seven right turns and can accommodate vehicles driving at speeds of up to 230 kilometers per hour in a straight line.
